name: nsgs
title: OCI Firewall — Network Security Groups
summary: Creates VNIC-level NSGs with standalone security rules supporting CIDR, service, and NSG-to-NSG references (within this invocation via `nsg_key`, or external via `external_nsg_id`).

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names and mandatory freeform tags.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Applied as the mandatory 'owner' freeform tag.

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code for chargeback/showback. Applied as the mandatory 'cost_center' freeform tag.

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Optional additional freeform tags merged beneath the mandatory tag set. Mandatory tags win on key collision.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: compartment_id
module.description: OCID of the compartment for the NSGs.

module.name: vcn_id
module.description: OCID of the VCN the NSGs belong to. Supplied by OCID — no framework dependency is implied.

module.name: network_security_groups
module.type: map(object({ rules = optional(map(object({ direction = string protocol = string description = optional(string) stateless = optional(bool, false) cidr = optional(string) service = optional(string) nsg_key = optional(string) external_nsg_id = optional(string) tcp_min = optional(number) tcp_max = optional(number) udp_min = optional(number) udp_max = optional(number) icmp_type = optional(number) icmp_code = optional(number) })), {}) }))
module.description: Map of NSGs keyed by logical name, each with a map of rules. nsg_key references another NSG in this map (OCI's analogue of AWS SG-to-SG rules); external_nsg_id references any pre-existing NSG. VNIC membership is declared on the VNIC/LB/etc. where the workload is managed — outside this module by design.

outputs.name: firewall_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical NSG name to network security group OCID. Reference these from VNICs, load balancers, and other NSG-capable resources.

outputs.name: rule_ids
outputs.description: Map of '<nsg-key>/<rule-key>' to security rule OCID.
